title: carya glabra subsp. odorata
intro: Carya glabra subsp. odorata, commonly known as the sweet pignut hickory, is a subspecies of deciduous tree native to eastern North America. The bark and leaves of this tree have been used in traditional herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of sweet pignut hickory include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and provide antioxidant protection. Sweet pignut hickory is typically prepared as a tea or infused in oil for its medicinal and herbal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
